channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 'I intend to kill Muslims': Indian mall worker in US stabbed by Utah man
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists cut climbing vines from a damaged Borneo forest. Its canopy then grew three times faster
item.description: Trimming aggressive climbing vines from logged tropical forests can substantially expedite canopy recovery, providing a more economical alternative to new tree planting for ecosystem restoration. Areas where vines were cut exhibited a carbon storage rate three times quicker than those with newly planted trees, as confirmed by laser mapping. Effective vine management promotes forestry sustainability and preserves biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: For nearly 50 years, China has placed straw grids across its deserts; desertified land is now shrinking by over 1,000 sq km a year, but scientists say the fight is far from over
item.description: China's Green Great Wall project has fought desertification for nearly fifty years. Millions of rural laborers have used straw checkerboards and planted saplings. This effort has reduced desertified land and increased forest cover significantly. The program demonstrates a long-term political commitment to environmental restoration. Sustaining these gains requires continued local involvement and adaptation.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Iran says 30 civilians killed in US strikes as Trump warns of 'hitting them harder'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Kick Argentina out': Why over 10 million people signed petition against Messi's team
item.description: An online petition demands Argentina's expulsion from the World Cup due to alleged FIFA favouritism. Millions have signed the petition, citing perceived bias towards Lionel Messi and his team. Critics point to controversial refereeing decisions and VAR reviews in several matches. Argentina's coach dismisses these claims, calling them baseless accusations from detractors. The winners of the Argentina-England semi-final will face Spain in the championship match.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This little girl has one adorable morning routine with her three Golden Retriever pet dogs
item.description: A little girl begins her day with morning cuddles for her three Golden Retrievers. She hugs Riley, Twyla, and Bear individually before any other activity. The dogs remain calm and affectionate during these daily morning greetings. This routine teaches the child about love and gentle connection. The heartwarming video captures a special bond between a child and her pets.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: British man jailed in Iran on spying charges given additional two-year sentence for speaking to media, family claims
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists warmed the same forest soil by 5°C for 37 years; in the fourth decade, microbes began releasing carbon once thought safely locked away
item.description: Forest soils are releasing more carbon than previously believed. Warming temperatures cause stable soil organic matter to decompose. This decomposition releases additional carbon dioxide into the atmosphere. The process creates a stronger climate feedback loop for global warming. Updated climate models will now account for this slow carbon release.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos cannot buy their way into this exclusive club in Florida
item.description: A wave of tech billionaires is transforming the real estate landscape on Miami's exclusive Indian Creek Island, snagging up luxury residences. However, simply owning a home here isn't enough to secure membership in the sought-after country club; one must be nominated and sanctioned by a board. This uptick in affluent individuals relocating to Florida from California stems primarily from concerns over impending wealth taxes in their former state.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Mike Tyson: "Fear is like fire. If you learn to control it, you let it work for you. If you don't…" - the boxing legend’s powerful lesson on controlling fear before it controls you
item.description: Mike Tyson viewed fear as a controllable force, much like fire. He believed controlled fear sharpens focus and aids preparation for challenges. Uncontrolled fear, however, leads to panic and poor decision-making. This perspective originated from his boxing mentor, Cus D'Amato, and shaped his career. Examining fear's signal, rather than hiding it, is key to its effective management.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Next week comes bridges, power plants': Trump says US strikes on Iran to escalate in three days
item.description: President Trump convened a meeting to discuss expanding military operations against Iran. The United States is currently conducting strikes targeting Iranian air defense systems. Trump warned of intensified attacks on power plants and bridges if Iran does not negotiate. Washington is also monitoring a deeply buried site believed to be part of Iran's nuclear program. Iran has continued retaliatory attacks on US military bases in the region.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Combine our strengths': EU, Ukraine sign multi-billion 'drone deal' to counter Russia
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Divers have just found the first silver bar from the Spanish shipwreck in nearly 30 years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Switzerland built giant tunnels under the Alps to reduce truck traffic, but the project is now helping protect the country's mountains from climate threats
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How elephants turn footsteps into messages
item.description: Through the ground, elephants send and receive vibrations, a unique way of communicating that surpasses traditional airborne sounds. Their anatomy, featuring sizable middle ear components and eardrums, allows for the efficient transfer of low-frequency signals to the cochlea. Intriguingly, they can also close their ear canals at will, amplifying their ability to perceive these low frequencies. This remarkable skill unveils the depths of their sophisticated communication systems.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A 1971 prediction by Stephen Hawking just came true with a cosmic collision 1.3 billion light-years away: Now scientists are trying to understand how
item.description: A cosmic collision of two black holes confirmed Stephen Hawking's fifty-year-old prediction. The observed increase in the merged black hole's area precisely matched theoretical calculations. This event provided the most accurate observational evidence for Hawking's black hole area law. Scientists collaborated across LIGO, Virgo, and KAGRA to analyze the gravitational wave signal. The precise data reinforces fundamental principles of modern physics and cosmology.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Saudi Arabia is building a giant car-free region beside the Red Sea, but its futuristic city plans have changed
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A Canadian entrepreneur sold his tech company for millions, then spent $1.5 million building tiny homes for people without homes
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Protection of children is state's responsibility': Pak lawmaker seeks social media ban for under-16s
item.description: A Pakistani lawmaker proposed banning social media accounts for children under sixteen. This resolution aims to protect minors from online dangers and psychological distress. The proposal urges federal legislation and parental consent for account creation. Pakistan's Telecommunication Authority must enforce age verification systems. This move aligns with global efforts to regulate children's social media use.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Saudi Arabia warns expats of SR50,000 fine, jail and deportation for overstaying entry visas
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
